title: **⭐ HIGH PRIORITY — Topic *Prepare* (Accepted→Prepared) has no UI affordance.** `POST /api/topics/{id}/prepare` (BL-036 / AC-035) shipped **backend-only**: the SPA never calls it (only `accept`/`return` are wired, in the kanban). So no topic reaches `Prepared`, the agenda-builder pool (`GET /topics?status=Prepared`) is **permanently empty in-product**, and "Search topics" shows nothing — the intake→agenda **core loop is broken for real users**. Discovered 2026-07-07 from an operator bug report; root-caused against `docs/domain/entity-lifecycles.md §1` (W4) + the design files (which omit Prepare entirely — the kanban has 5 buckets, no `Prepared`; the timeline jumps Accepted→Scheduled).
activation_trigger: **Activate immediately.** Fix (frontend-only, no ADR): `usePrepareTopic` + a **"Mark prepared"** button on an `Accepted` topic detail (`TopicDetail.tsx` `.dt-actions`); **gating = show-and-enforce** (render for any Accepted topic, backend 403s — the owner is often a plain Member, so a role gate would hide it from the right person); **invalidate three query keys** (backlog + `['topics','prepared']` + `['topics','detail',key]`); a **"Prepared" badge** on kanban cards (Accepted & Prepared share the `accepted` bucket, `topicMeta.ts`, so Prepared is otherwise invisible); reword the agenda-pool empty-state to point to the action. **Optional W4-completeness:** notify the Secretary on prepare (`PrepareTopicHandler` only audits today; the `INotificationChannel` seam exists). **Open sub-questions (raise an OQ if pursued):** the domain has **no "un-prepare" / defer-from-Prepared** transition (a mis-prepared topic is stuck until scheduled); and **design-update-owed** (the design omits Prepare). **✅ RESOLVED 2026-07-09 (Tier 3):** "Mark prepared" button on the Accepted-topic detail (`usePrepareTopic`, show-and-enforce, inline 403) + Prepared kanban badge + reworded agenda empty-state + `PrepareTopicHandler` Secretary notification (skip-self); `core-loop.spec` now **clicks the button** instead of a direct-HTTP prepare (the swapped spec was run locally against the live stack — green; CI re-validates on the PR), and `TopicApiTests` proves the new DI seams. Un-prepare gap → **OQ-049** (default = defer). Design-update-owed still stands.

title: Audit immutability + verify hardening: (1) a **DB-permission grant** that makes the `audit` schema INSERT/SELECT-only (block UPDATE/DELETE for out-of-app / DBA writes), and (2) a **nightly Hangfire chain-verify job** (C-INS-02) that runs `GET /api/audit/verify` on a schedule and alerts on a broken link.

AC-017/018/019/020 are `Met` on this basis (ADR-0026/0027, PR #105).", "Target phase": "Phase 2 (P16 hardening)", "Trigger to activate": "P16 security-hardening slice", "Status": "**Done in-repo 2026-07-16 (P16a); DB-permission = Operator/P18 residual.** (2) Nightly Hangfire chain-verify **shipped** as `IIntegrityVerifier` (`Acmp.Worker` `Cron.Daily(3)`) — verifies the audit chain **and** every sealed vote's ballot chain + tally, alerting via Serilog + a durable `AuditEvent` (C-INS-02, ADR-0030). (1) DB-permission grant **shipped** as migration `Audit_DenyMutation` (`DENY UPDATE,DELETE ON SCHEMA::audit TO acmp_app`, ADR-0031), proven by `AuditImmutabilityDbPermissionTests` — **CLOSED P18a (2026-07-19): the runtime now connects as the least-priv `acmp_svc` login (ADR-0031/0032; datawriter+acmp_app, non-sysadmin), so the DENY is effective — the extended test proves audit UPDATE/DELETE is DB-denied for that exact principal. D-16 fully done.**"}}

title: Enriched before/after for **owned-child** audit rows. Child ops (e.g. Research add/verify Finding, add/set-status Recommendation) emit an `AuditEvent` (INV-005 holds) but with **empty before/after**: `AuditCaptureInterceptor` captures only mutated `AuditableEntity` rows, and owned children (`Finding`/`Recommendation`) are `BaseEntity`, while the parent aggregate's scalars are unchanged on a child add. Enhancement = make owned children `AuditableEntity` (audit columns → migration) + emit the child-op audit with the **child** as subject (`FND-`/`REC-`) so the capture correlates.

title: Audit-append **concurrency contention.** `SqlAuditSink` takes no application-level lock: two genuinely concurrent state-changing requests both read the same chain tip (`TipHashAsync`) and race the `audit.AuditEvents` `PreviousHash` UNIQUE index — the loser gets SQL error 2601 (duplicate key) and the whole operation **500s** (fail-closed, but a hard error rather than a retry). Accepted at design time (ADR-0009/0026 + the `SqlAuditSink.cs` `ponytail:` note — "negligible at ≤20-user on-prem scale; if it ever bites, serialize via `sp_getapplock` or retry"). **Reproduced 2026-07-13** by the P15b live VR: the SPA's login-time `Membership.ProfileSynced` append raced an immediate seed burst, so one `Research.MissionProposed` lost the race and 500'd (audit seq 12/13 both chained off tip `1f738021`). Fix (the pre-named upgrade path): a **bounded retry** on 2601/2627 inside `AppendAsync` — re-read tip, recompute hash, re-insert — which preserves chain linearity + the fail-closed guarantee; or `sp_getapplock` serialization. **2026-07-13 — operator chose to fix now**, then (after adversarial review) to fix it **robustly** rather than cheaply. Arc: a first pass added a bounded optimistic-retry in `SqlAuditSink`; CI then showed the 8-way stress case **deadlocks (SQL 1205)**, whose victim's transaction is rolled back → in-place retry cannot recover. A devil's-advocate review further found a **sink-level app lock is lock-order-unsafe** (handlers like `IssueDecision`/`VerifyAction` emit-then-write-more, inverting `{lock, module-rows}` order). **✅ RESOLVED 2026-07-13 via ADR-0028 (serialize at tx-open):** `AmbientTransaction.EnsureStartedAsync` takes a **transaction-scoped exclusive `sp_getapplock`** ('acmp-audit-chain') immediately after `BeginTransaction` — before any module write — so every audited write-command acquires the chain lock first (one consistent order → no cross-handler deadlock) and appends serialize (no fork, no 1205). Held to commit ⇒ next command reads a committed tip; `THROW` on lock-timeout ⇒ fail-closed; SQL-Server-only (degrades to no-op elsewhere). The **denial/autocommit path** (no ambient tx) keeps the bounded retry as its backstop (broadened to catch the `PreviousHash` **or** `Hash` index race; deadlock-free — no crosswise module locks). Tests: `Concurrent_commands_all_commit_without_forking_the_audit_chain` (8 write-commands serialize) + `Concurrent_denials_all_record_their_audit_via_the_retry_without_forking_the_chain` (8 denials converge via retry) + the same-tx atomicity suite, all green (CI-proven for the write-command deadlock case). No schema/migration.

title: **Flaky frontend test — `src/Acmp.Web/src/features/decisions/DecisionPage.test.tsx:174`.** Intermittently reads `/decisions/DECN-2026-008` where `/decisions/DECN-2026-015` is expected; unrelated red on the D-18 PR #111 CI run 2026-07-13, green on the immediate re-run. **⚠ The original diagnosis recorded here was wrong** and is corrected in place (P17a, 2026-07-17): it blamed "non-deterministic decision selection (order-/hash-dependent pick over the mocked decisions list)" — **there is no decisions list and no selection in that test.** `useDecision` is mocked to one fixed object (`:54-56`) and the supersede mutation is a fixed `mockResolvedValue({ key: 'DECN-2026-015' })` (`:78`); both are deterministic. **Real mechanism:** `DECN-2026-008` is simply `setup()`'s initial route (`:62`), and `DECN-2026-015` is reachable only *after* `SupersedeDialog.onConfirm` awaits `mutateAsync` and then calls `navigate()` (`SupersedeDialog.tsx:70-81`). The assertion read `expect(await screen.findByTestId('loc')).toHaveTextContent(...)` — but `loc` (`LocationDisplay`) is **always mounted**, so `findByTestId` resolved on its first poll without waiting for anything, and `toHaveTextContent` then evaluated **exactly once** against a pathname the post-await `navigate()` may not yet have flushed. Hence the initial route, and hence why a re-run passes. **Scope note:** the sibling sync assertions at `:168`/`:188`/`:209`/`:217-220` are **not** affected — `mutateAsync` is *invoked* synchronously inside `onConfirm`, so an awaited `user.click` guarantees the call happened; only code after the handler's own `await` races. They were deliberately left untouched.
activation_trigger: **✅ Done 2026-07-17 (P17a)** — retry the *assertion* rather than the element: `await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByTestId('loc')).toHaveTextContent('/decisions/DECN-2026-015'))`. `:235` left as-is (sync `getByTestId` asserting the path *stays* on the error path — correct there, and preceded by a real `findByText` await).

title: **Confidentiality ABAC (C-AUTHZ-04) — `Restricted` topics.** `security-controls.md` C-AUTHZ-04 specifies a `ConfidentialityRequirement` ABAC handler restricting `Restricted` topics (security findings, sensitive partner matters) to Chair/Coord + explicit grantees + Owner/Assignees + Auditor, excluded from default stream visibility **and search results**. title: **Inline `style={{}}` usage in the SPA — style hygiene, NOT a security control.** ~110 inline-style sites across ~25 components (18 static, 7 dynamic + `SortableList.tsx`'s @dnd-kit drag transform). P16-B3 planned to refactor them all (static → CSS classes, dynamic → a `useCssVars` CSSOM hook, plus a @dnd-kit CSSOM shim) in order to drop `style-src 'unsafe-inline'`. **Verified 2026-07-16 that the refactor is unnecessary for that goal:** CSP governs `<style>` elements and `style=""` attributes, not the CSSOM, and react-dom applies the `style` prop via CSSOM writes (`style.setProperty(...)` / `style[name] = v` — confirmed in the react-dom source), never serialising a style attribute. With no SSR (client-rendered Vite SPA), no inline style in `index.html`, DOMPurify's allowlist excluding `style` attrs + `<style>` tags, and no CSS-in-JS dependency, `style-src 'self'` ships **with the code as-is** (browser-verified: CSSOM writes apply, `setAttribute('style')` and injected `<style>` are blocked). C-WEB-02 is therefore **Met without this work**.
activation_trigger: A frontend consistency/design-system pass elects to take it on. **Do not activate for CSP reasons** — that rationale is disproven.
